items.title: No dry-run mode for cmdPhaseRemove and cmdMilestoneComplete
items.description: lib/phase.js (cmdPhaseRemove ~line 500+, cmdMilestoneComplete ~line 776+): these destructive operations have no --dry-run flag. With --force or yolo mode, they execute immediately with no preview. The evolve discovery engine itself lists 'missing-dry-run-support' as a standard issue category. Add --dry-run that shows what would be changed without committing.

items.title: No structured logging — all diagnostics use raw process.stderr.write
items.description: lib/evolve.js line 1295, lib/autopilot.js lines 134-135, lib/worktree.js various: diagnostic output uses raw `process.stderr.write(...)` with no log level concept, no structured format, and no way to suppress verbose output. spawnClaude in lib/autopilot.js line 134 forwards ALL subprocess stdout to parent stdout, polluting JSON output in MCP contexts. Add a `debug(msg)` helper gated on GRD_DEBUG env var and a `warn(msg)` for non-fatal issues.

items.title: Evolve loop can repeatedly select the same failed work items indefinitely
items.description: lib/evolve.js lines 1363+ and 251-289: the DIMENSION_WEIGHTS scoring selects highest-scored pending items. Failed items are moved back to 'pending' in advanceIteration(), so the same item can be selected, attempted, fail, and be selected again indefinitely. Add a failure counter per work item or a minimum iteration gap before re-selection to prevent infinite loops on impossible improvements.

items.title: No public API to reset module-level caches between autopilot iterations
items.description: lib/gates.js and lib/cleanup.js expose no reset functions for their module-level caches. The autopilot loop in lib/autopilot.js calls cmdPhaseComplete for each phase but cannot invalidate stale file content cached from the previous phase. Export resetGatesCache() and resetCleanupCache() and call them at the start of each autopilot iteration.

items.title: No schema validation for .planning/config.json
items.description: bin/grd-tools.js loads .planning/config.json with no JSON schema validation. Unknown or misspelled keys (e.g., 'autonomous_mode' vs the correct 'yolo' toggle) are silently ignored. A JSON Schema validation step at startup would catch configuration errors early.

items.title: No grd-tools health-check subcommand
items.description: bin/grd-tools.js has no 'health' or 'doctor' command to verify that .planning/ directory structure is consistent, required files exist, git is configured, and node version meets requirements. This would help onboarding and post-update diagnostics.

items.title: No undo/rollback for phase operations
items.description: lib/phase.js phase add/insert/remove modify ROADMAP.md in place with no backup or undo mechanism. A backup of ROADMAP.md before mutation (e.g., .planning/ROADMAP.md.bak) and a 'phase undo' command would allow recovery from mistakes.

items.title: No structured JSON output for CI/monitoring integration
items.description: bin/grd-tools.js 'progress' command outputs human-readable text but has no --json flag for the overall project status summary. CI pipelines and dashboards cannot parse the current output to track phase completion rates or blocked items.

items.title: buildDependencyGraph not exposed as CLI command
items.description: lib/deps.js — buildDependencyGraph function exists but no CLI command wraps it. Users can't visualize phase dependencies, detect cycles, or plan parallel execution from the command line.

items.title: No rollback/undo for destructive state operations
items.description: N/A (feature gap) — Once a phase is removed or STATE.md patched, no way to revert except manual git reset. Accidental destructive operations are hard to recover from without a snapshot/restore mechanism.

items.title: ROADMAP.md has no phase dependency declarations
items.description: lib/roadmap.js — Phase descriptions can't express 'Phase 5 depends on Phase 3 completion'. No way to mark phases as blockers. Autopilot may execute phases out of dependency order.

items.title: No aggregate project health score or trend analysis
items.description: lib/commands.js — Coverage, test results, and lint issues are checked separately. No aggregate health score or trend analysis across milestone progression to see if code quality is improving over time.

items.title: No machine-readable API spec for 50+ CLI commands
items.description: N/A (feature gap) — GRD exposes 50+ CLI commands and MCP tools with no OpenAPI/machine-readable specification. External tools can't discover available operations; no type safety guarantees.

items.title: No phase template system for common phase types
items.description: lib/scaffold.js — Phase creation requires manual file creation with no built-in templates for common types (research, implementation, evaluation). Users write boilerplate instead of meaningful content.

items.title: cmdPhaseRemove has no --dry-run preview mode for the destructive renumber operation
items.description: lib/phase.js (cmdPhaseRemove) — phase removal permanently deletes directories and renumbers all subsequent phases. There is no `--dry-run` flag to show what would be deleted and renumbered before committing. Adding one would prevent accidental data loss.

items.title: Evolve loop permanently drops failed work items with no retry mechanism
items.description: lib/evolve.js line 251 — `advanceIteration` does not carry `state.failed` items into the next iteration's `remaining` list. A transient Claude API error permanently marks an item as failed. There is no retry logic, backoff, or user-configurable max-retry count.

items.title: cmdHealthCheck and cmdCoverageReport are exported but have no unit tests
items.description: lib/commands.js (cmdHealthCheck, cmdCoverageReport) — these commands perform filesystem operations and produce structured JSON output that is fully testable, but neither commands.test.js nor the integration tests cover them. They should be added to the test suite to maintain per-file coverage thresholds.

items.title: No phase dependency tracking — phases can execute out of order
items.description: ROADMAP.md phases have no dependency declarations. If a user runs /grd:execute-phase 3 before phase 2 is complete, there's no guard. Add optional depends_on: [1, 2] frontmatter to PLAN.md files and a pre-execution check in lib/phase.js that warns (or blocks in non-YOLO mode) when prerequisites are incomplete.

items.title: No rollback mechanism when phase execution fails mid-way
items.description: lib/phase.js cmdPhaseComplete and worktree.js execute plans but have no undo path. When git.enabled=true and a plan fails after partial file writes, the worktree is left in a dirty state. Add a rollback command that resets the worktree to the pre-execution HEAD and marks the plan as failed in STATE.md.

items.title: config.json has no schema validation on load
items.description: bin/grd-tools.js loads .planning/config.json via JSON.parse but doesn't validate it against a schema. Unknown keys, wrong value types (e.g., ceremony.default_level: 'full' vs 'invalid'), or missing required fields only manifest as cryptic downstream errors. Add a JSON Schema or Zod validation step at load time with clear error messages.

items.title: No structured logging — debugging requires adding console.log
items.description: The codebase has no debug logging facility. When operations fail silently (see empty catch blocks), there's no way to enable verbose output without modifying code. Add a GRD_DEBUG=1 environment variable that routes to a debug logger, replacing the 37 silent catches with conditional debug.log() calls.

items.title: MCP Atlassian tracker allows scheduling without a plan timeline URL
items.description: lib/tracker.js lines 1–50: Date scheduling for Jira Plans can be triggered without validating that `plan_timeline_url` is present. This creates orphaned schedule entries in Jira that aren't linked to any timeline view.

items.title: cmdPhaseAdd accepts empty description string
items.description: bin/grd-tools.js lines 340–341: Validation only checks `if (!description)`, which passes for a single space or other blank strings. Phases with whitespace-only descriptions create malformed ROADMAP.md entries that break downstream parsing.

items.title: cmdPhaseInsert allows duplicate phase names
items.description: lib/phase.js lines 175–183: No validation checks whether the new phase description matches an existing phase name. Duplicate names create ambiguity in tracker sync, worktree branch names, and verification reports.

items.title: analyzeCodebaseForItems eagerly loads all lib/ files into memory
items.description: lib/evolve.js lines 340–350: All source files are read into memory upfront even when a discoverer function doesn't need them. On large codebases this wastes significant RAM. Files should be loaded lazily on demand.

items.title: Add a grd-tools doctor command for project health checking
items.description: There is no built-in way to diagnose a broken GRD project setup. A `grd-tools doctor` command could check: config.json validity, ROADMAP.md parsability, STATE.md structure, phase directory/roadmap consistency, missing required files, and outdated milestone archives. Would surface issues before they cause confusing failures.

items.title: Add structured JSON logging mode for CI/programmatic use
items.description: All CLI output is plain text or JSON-per-command but there is no way to get a structured event log across a full operation (e.g., all steps of execute-phase). A `--log-json` global flag emitting newline-delimited JSON events would make GRD scriptable and easier to integrate with observability tools.

items.title: Add --dry-run flag to destructive commands
items.description: Commands like `phase remove`, `milestone complete`, and `roadmap` mutations have no dry-run mode. Users can't preview what will change before committing. Add `--dry-run` to these commands to print planned changes without writing files.

items.title: No migration system for .planning/config.json schema changes
items.description: When GRD adds new config fields (e.g., ceremony.phase_overrides, git.worktree_dir), existing projects get undefined for those fields until manually updated. Add a config migration system (like database migrations) that auto-applies schema updates on load and logs what was changed.

items.title: No history log for STATE.md changes to enable rollback
items.description: bin/grd-tools.js state patch and state advance-plan modify STATE.md directly with no history. If a state update corrupts the file (e.g., during an interrupted evolve loop), there is no way to recover the previous state. Add append-only .planning/STATE.log with timestamped diffs.

items.title: Phase add/insert/remove have no --dry-run mode
items.description: lib/phase.js cmdPhaseAdd, cmdPhaseInsert, and cmdPhaseRemove modify ROADMAP.md and the filesystem with no preview mode. A --dry-run flag would show what changes will be made before committing, reducing risk of mistakes on multi-phase roadmaps.

items.title: No CLI command to validate .planning/config.json
items.description: Users can misconfigure .planning/config.json (wrong tracker type, invalid ceremony level, bad model profile) and only discover errors at runtime deep in a workflow. A grd-tools.js validate config (or /grd:settings --validate) command would catch misconfigurations early.

items.title: No formal phase dependency graph or blocking relationships
items.description: ROADMAP.md has no mechanism to declare that phase N must complete before phase M can start. The autopilot runs phases sequentially by number, but for projects where phases are truly independent, there's no way to express or enforce actual dependencies. This would enable better parallelism decisions.

items.title: grd progress output lacks visual trend indicators
items.description: bin/grd-tools.js progress command outputs a table/bar but has no trend data (is this phase faster or slower than average?). Adding sparkline metrics from SUMMARY.md execution durations would let users spot velocity changes across phases at a glance.

items.title: Replace process.exit calls in commands.js
items.description: lib/commands.js uses process.exit() directly. Use the error() utility function instead for consistent error handling.

items.title: Replace process.exit calls in evolve.js
items.description: lib/evolve.js uses process.exit() directly. Use the error() utility function instead for consistent error handling.

items.title: Replace process.exit calls in mcp-server.js
items.description: lib/mcp-server.js uses process.exit() directly. Use the error() utility function instead for consistent error handling.

items.title: Replace process.exit calls in roadmap.js
items.description: lib/roadmap.js uses process.exit() directly. Use the error() utility function instead for consistent error handling.

items.title: Replace process.exit calls in state.js
items.description: lib/state.js uses process.exit() directly. Use the error() utility function instead for consistent error handling.

items.title: Replace process.exit calls in utils.js
items.description: lib/utils.js uses process.exit() directly. Use the error() utility function instead for consistent error handling.

items.title: 14 of 19 lib/ modules missing 'use strict'
items.description: CLAUDE.md requires `'use strict'` at the top of every file. It is missing from: lib/state.js, lib/roadmap.js, lib/phase.js, lib/tracker.js, lib/commands.js, lib/utils.js, lib/context.js, lib/verify.js, lib/gates.js, lib/cleanup.js, lib/scaffold.js, lib/deps.js, lib/parallel.js, lib/frontmatter.js. Only lib/worktree.js, lib/evolve.js, lib/autopilot.js, lib/markdown-split.js, and lib/paths.js include it.

items.title: lib/state.js cmdStateLoad uses direct process.exit instead of output()
items.description: lib/state.js lines 85-89: inside cmdStateLoad, when `raw` is true, the function calls `process.stdout.write(lines.join('\n')); process.exit(0)` directly, bypassing the `output()` utility used consistently everywhere else in the file. The output() utility at lib/utils.js lines 498-505 handles both JSON and raw formats. This direct call breaks the MCP captureExecution wrapper.

items.title: verify-summary command skips subcommand validation
items.description: bin/grd-tools.js line 40 imports cmdVerifySummary and uses it at line 305, but `verify-summary` is not listed in VERIFY_SUBS at line 175. All other verify subcommands go through validateSubcommand(). This inconsistency means verify-summary gets no input validation while all sibling commands do. Fix: either add it to VERIFY_SUBS or make it a proper subcommand of `verify`.

items.title: Phase directory lookup uses startsWith which can match wrong phases
items.description: lib/phase.js lines 74-80: `dirs.find((d) => d.startsWith(normalized))` is ambiguous — filtering for phase '0' would match '01', '02', '06', etc. The correct pattern (used in lib/commands.js line 1329) is `d.startsWith(normalized + '-') || d === normalized`. The same weak pattern exists in lib/utils.js line 557 in findPhaseInternal.

items.title: Tracker mapping uses unpadded phase keys inconsistently
items.description: lib/tracker.js lines 96-100, 532, 762: `mapping.phases[String(phaseNum)]` at line 532 uses unpadded keys, but normalizePhaseName produces zero-padded phase numbers. The mapping table may be keyed either way depending on how it was written, causing lookup misses. Standardize all tracker mapping keys to use the normalized (padded) phase number.

items.title: flag() helper duplicated in bin/grd-tools.js instead of shared in utils
items.description: bin/grd-tools.js lines 131-134 defines a local `flag(args, name, fallback)` helper and the same pattern is replicated inline throughout the command switch cases. This utility belongs in lib/utils.js alongside parseIncludeFlag so it can be imported in both bin/ and lib/ files without duplication.

items.title: YAML frontmatter parsed with regex instead of yaml library
items.description: lib/frontmatter.js uses regex-based YAML parsing throughout. Quoted values, multi-line strings, and escaped characters are not handled correctly. The js-yaml package is already a common Node.js dependency and would be more robust.

items.title: STATE.md fields extracted/replaced via fragile regex
items.description: lib/state.js lines 22-42, stateExtractField and stateReplaceField use regex to parse the living STATE.md. Fields containing regex special characters (e.g., URLs with `?` or `.`) could cause silent data corruption.

items.title: Tracker config migration mixed with parsing logic
items.description: lib/tracker.js lines 28-89, loadTrackerConfig() both parses and auto-migrates legacy formats (jira → mcp-atlassian) in the same function. Migration logic should be a separate migrateTrackerConfig() step called once at load time.

items.title: Git worktree output parsed with fragile string manipulation
items.description: lib/worktree.js lines 109-137, parsePorcelainWorktrees() manually splits git porcelain output. This is fragile across git versions. Using `git worktree list --porcelain` with a structured parser or `--format` option would be more reliable.

items.title: Command .md files missing description frontmatter field
items.description: All 42 files in commands/ directory are missing a 'description:' YAML frontmatter field. The description field enables CLI help text and command discovery. Without it, commands are not self-documenting and tooling cannot introspect them.

items.title: Three different config loading patterns across modules
items.description: lib/utils.js:100-250, lib/tracker.js:10-80, lib/backend.js:40-100 — Each module implements config loading differently: utils.js merges defaults, tracker.js handles migrations, backend.js uses raw fs.readFileSync. Config overrides work differently depending on which module calls loadConfig.

items.title: Inconsistent state file operation patterns
items.description: lib/state.js:50-200 and lib/evolve.js:100-200 — state.js uses regex field extraction while evolve.js uses JSON parse. No unified abstraction creates different TOCTOU vulnerability windows across code paths.

items.title: Inconsistent JSON output envelopes across commands
items.description: Various files in commands.js, state.js, phase.js, tracker.js — Some commands return { result: {...} }, others return flat objects, others use custom keys like { healthy: true }. Scripts parsing output must handle multiple formats.

items.title: Frontmatter fields not validated against schema
items.description: lib/frontmatter.js:20-100 — extractFrontmatter returns JavaScript object without validating field types. Invalid values (e.g., wrong verification_level, malformed duration) silently coerce and crash downstream code.

items.title: Hardcoded .worktrees/ path not always using config
items.description: lib/worktree.js:50-150 — Some code paths still reference .worktrees/ directly rather than computing path from config. If user configures custom worktree location, inconsistent state can result.

items.title: 17 lib/ files are missing 'use strict' required by CLAUDE.md
items.description: CLAUDE.md mandates `'use strict'` at the top of every file, but only 5 of 22 lib/bin files comply (worktree.js, paths.js, autopilot.js, evolve.js, markdown-split.js). Files missing it include: state.js, commands.js, roadmap.js, frontmatter.js, scaffold.js, phase.js, tracker.js, cleanup.js, context.js, deps.js, parallel.js, long-term-roadmap.js, backend.js, gates.js, verify.js, utils.js.

items.title: Direct process.exit() calls in lib/ functions bypass the output() pattern
items.description: lib/commands.js lines 1219, 1292, 1310, 1339, 1590, 1824 and lib/state.js line 88 call `process.exit()` directly instead of using `output()`. The MCP server must intercept `process.exit` to prevent crashes (mcp-server.js lines 1885-1920); any new function that calls it directly will silently crash the MCP server.

items.title: cmdStateLoad raw path uses process.stdout.write + process.exit instead of output()
items.description: lib/state.js lines 87-89 — the raw-mode path calls `process.stdout.write(lines.join('\n'))` then `process.exit(0)` directly, bypassing the `output()` utility that all other cmd* functions use. This makes the raw path inconsistent and harder to test.

items.title: autopilot.js uses hardcoded .planning/ paths that should use lib/paths.js
items.description: lib/autopilot.js lines 247, 266, 330 hardcode `.planning/autopilot`, `.planning/STATE.md`, and `.planning/autopilot/autopilot.log`. lib/paths.js exists to centralize these paths. The autopilot directory structure is invisible to path-consistency checks.

items.title: evolve.js hardcoded-path scan excludes itself but not autopilot.js which violates the same rule
items.description: lib/evolve.js line 640 excludes `evolve.js` from the hardcoded-path scan, but `autopilot.js` (lines 247, 266, 330) also uses hardcoded `.planning/` paths and is not excluded. The evolve discovery engine won't flag autopilot.js for a pattern it explicitly enforces.

items.title: 331 ad-hoc path.join calls bypass lib/paths.js helpers
items.description: lib/paths.js exposes getPhasesDirPath(), getMilestonePath(), etc. but many modules call path.join directly with the same string literals. lib/phase.js alone has 40+ raw path.join calls. Inconsistency means changes to the directory layout require grep-and-pray updates rather than a single helper change.

items.title: Mixed error-handling strategies across modules
items.description: Some functions throw via the error() helper (lib/commands.js), some via new Error(), some call process.exit(), and some silently swallow. lib/postinstall.js:61 uses process.stderr.write directly. Establish a single convention (e.g., throw GrdError with a code, handled at CLI boundary) and enforce it.

items.title: CLI output mixes JSON, plain text, and markdown inconsistently
items.description: bin/grd-tools.js commands use --raw for plain text and default to JSON, but lib/commands.js writes directly to stdout with ANSI colors and markdown formatting, bypassing the JSON layer. Some command outputs mix both. Define clear output contracts: machine-readable JSON for grd-tools, styled markdown for commands.

items.title: Slug generation logic duplicated across phase.js and utils.js
items.description: lib/utils.js exports a slug() function but lib/phase.js implements its own inline slug-like normalization for phase directory names (lowercase, replace spaces with dashes). The two implementations may differ for edge cases like Unicode characters or multiple consecutive dashes. Consolidate to one implementation.

items.title: Field-name regex duplicated across stateExtractField and cmdStateGet
items.description: lib/state.js lines 22–42 vs 113–134: `stateExtractField`, `stateReplaceField`, and `cmdStateGet` each maintain their own copy of the field-matching regex. A format change requires three edits and the discrepancy is a latent bug.

items.title: Template placeholder replacement duplicated in worktreeBranch and milestoneBranch
items.description: lib/worktree.js lines 47–87: Both functions implement identical string-template interpolation. Changing the template format requires synchronized edits in two places.

items.title: Inconsistent catch-block documentation across phase.js
items.description: lib/phase.js lines 339–341, 414, 474: Some empty catch blocks have an explanatory comment ('phases directory doesn't exist') while others are bare. The inconsistency makes it hard to distinguish intentional suppression from accidental oversight during reviews.

items.title: template fill uses --fields JSON while all other commands use individual flags
items.description: bin/grd-tools.js lines 194–206: `template fill --fields '{...}'` accepts a raw JSON string, but every other command accepts individual named flags. This breaks the principle of least surprise for CLI users.

items.title: GitHub and MCP-specific tracker code mixed in tracker.js
items.description: lib/tracker.js lines 1–1035: Provider-specific code for GitHub Issues and MCP Atlassian is interleaved without a common interface. Adding a third provider (e.g., Linear) requires scattered edits across the entire file.

items.title: Inconsistent error handling: some functions throw, others return error objects
items.description: lib/utils.js and lib/commands.js use mixed patterns — some functions throw exceptions on invalid input, others return `{error: 'message'}` objects. This inconsistency forces callers to handle both patterns. Standardize: lib/ functions should throw; commands.js catches and formats.

items.title: Complex functions lack JSDoc (buildDependencyGraph, extractFrontmatter, computeSchedule)
items.description: lib/deps.js `buildDependencyGraph()`, lib/frontmatter.js `extractFrontmatter()`, and lib/roadmap.js `computeSchedule()` have no JSDoc. These are the most complex functions in the codebase. Add @param/@returns/@example doc comments for each.

items.title: THEME_PATTERNS in evolve.js is hardcoded with no extension mechanism
items.description: lib/evolve.js lines 49-72: 25 hardcoded theme patterns with no way to add project-specific themes. New themes require code changes. Move to `.planning/config.json` with a `evolve.themes` array, merging with the default set.

items.title: verify.js file pattern matching has hardcoded extensions
items.description: lib/verify.js lines 60-84: file pattern matching uses 2 hardcoded patterns that miss `.tsx`, `.py`, and other source file types. Make the extension list configurable or use a project-aware glob pattern.

items.title: CLI help text embedded in error() calls instead of centralized registry
items.description: lib/commands.js: usage text is embedded in individual error() calls throughout the file, making it hard to keep consistent or extract for documentation generation. Create a COMMAND_HELP object mapping command names to {usage, description, examples}.

items.title: Three incompatible error handling patterns used across lib/ modules
items.description: Modules inconsistently use: (1) error() function causing process.exit, (2) return {error: string} objects, (3) thrown exceptions. For example lib/tracker.js uses error() while lib/phase.js returns error objects and lib/evolve.js throws. Standardize: libraries return {error} objects, CLI entry points call error().

items.title: Phase number variable named inconsistently across modules
items.description: The phase number variable is named phaseNum in some files, phase in others, phaseNumber in others, and n in yet others. Inconsistency makes grep-based navigation and refactoring harder. Standardize on phaseNum throughout lib/ and bin/.

items.title: Git operation utilities duplicated across lib/worktree.js and lib/autopilot.js
items.description: lib/worktree.js uses execGit() wrapper for branch operations while lib/autopilot.js calls spawnSync('git', ...) directly. These should be consolidated into a single git utility module (lib/git.js or lib/utils.js) to avoid divergent error handling and argument patterns.

items.title: CLI command functions return void, objects, or null inconsistently
items.description: lib/commands.js cmdPhaseAdd() returns void (output via stdout), createTracker() returns object or null, discoverWithClaude() returns object or throws. No documented return pattern for command functions makes them hard to compose. Document and standardize return contracts per function category.

items.title: STATE.md field matching is case-insensitive, hiding typos
items.description: lib/state.js matches STATE.md fields case-insensitively ('Current Phase', 'Current plan', 'Stopped at', 'Stopped At' are all treated equivalently). This hides user typos and makes field names non-authoritative. Should enforce a strict schema with canonical casing.

items.title: Mixed function naming conventions across lib/ modules
items.description: lib/ modules use a mix of cmd* (cmdPhaseAdd), get* (getPhaseDir), analyze* (analyzeComplexity), and _internal* prefixes without consistent conventions. It's unclear which functions are public API vs internal helpers. Should establish and document a naming convention.

items.title: 15+ process.exit() calls scattered through CLI modules
items.description: bin/grd-tools.js, lib/utils.js, and lib/commands.js contain 15+ direct calls to process.exit(0/1). This makes unit testing difficult (tests can't catch logic before exit) and integration tests must be used for basic coverage. Wrap in a testable exit callback.

items.title: lib/commands.js is 3,014 lines — unmanageably large
items.description: lib/commands.js contains 8+ logically distinct command families: dashboard/TUI rendering, requirement management, search, migration, health check, coverage report, long-term roadmap, and quality analysis. It is nearly 3x larger than the next largest file. Natural split points: lib/tui.js or lib/display.js for rendering functions, lib/requirements.js for requirement operations, keeping only lightweight dispatch functions in commands.js.

items.title: analyzeRoadmap() calls readdirSync inside a per-phase loop
items.description: lib/roadmap.js lines 370-391: inside the `while ((match = phasePattern.exec(...)))` loop at line 344, each iteration calls `fs.readdirSync(phasesDir)`. For a project with 40 phases, this is 40 identical readdirSync calls on the same directory. Hoist the call out of the loop — read the directory once before the loop and reuse the result.

items.title: ROADMAP.md parsed twice: computeSchedule + analyzeRoadmap both parse it independently
items.description: lib/roadmap.js lines 52-148 and 344-412: computeSchedule() and analyzeRoadmap() each parse milestones and phases from ROADMAP.md independently. analyzeRoadmap() then calls computeSchedule() to enrich phases with dates, causing two full parses. The milestoneRegex in computeSchedule (line 73) and milestonePattern in analyzeRoadmap (line 416) are nearly identical. Refactor computeSchedule to accept pre-parsed data or merge schedule computation into analyzeRoadmap.

items.title: cmdDashboard re-parses ROADMAP.md instead of using analyzeRoadmap()
items.description: lib/commands.js lines 1025-1210: the dashboard defines its own parseDashboardPhases, parseDashboardActiveMilestones, parseDashboardShippedMilestones, and parseDashboardStateSummary functions (several hundred lines), largely duplicating what analyzeRoadmap() in lib/roadmap.js already computes. Using analyzeRoadmap() as the data source and adding dashboard-specific formatting on top would eliminate hundreds of lines of duplicate parsing logic.

items.title: context.js is 1377 lines with 20+ init functions
items.description: lib/context.js is 1377 lines containing 20+ cmdInit* functions each 50-100 lines. The init functions could be grouped into domain sub-modules (phase-init.js, research-init.js) to improve navigability and testability.

items.title: MCP server has 1000+ lines of inline tool definitions
items.description: lib/mcp-server.js is 2227 lines and contains the COMMAND_DESCRIPTORS table inline alongside registration logic. The declarative tool definitions should be extracted to a separate data file for easier maintenance.

items.title: analyzeComplexity() spawns ESLint on every call without caching
items.description: lib/cleanup.js lines 62-100, analyzeComplexity() spawns an ESLint subprocess each call with no caching. Phase completion can trigger multiple cleanup analyses; results should be memoized per file path.

items.title: Duplicate run-cache pattern in gates.js and cleanup.js
items.description: lib/gates.js lines 17-23 defines _gatesRunCache and lib/cleanup.js defines _cleanupRunCache using the same in-memory Map caching pattern. A shared createRunCache() utility in utils.js would eliminate duplication.

items.title: commands.js is a 3,014-line monolith with 45 functions
items.description: lib/commands.js — Single module handles slug generation, todos, config, history, progress, requirements, coverage, and health checks. Logically separable into 5-6 domain modules. Hard to locate functionality and changes risk cross-command interference.

items.title: cleanup.js quality analysis engine is 1,349 lines
items.description: lib/cleanup.js:70-300 — analyzeComplexity, findDeadExports, checkDocDrift, testCoverageGaps all in one file with duplicated ESLint subprocess spawning logic. Hard to test individual analysis types.

items.title: evolve.js mixes state management with discovery logic
items.description: lib/evolve.js:200-600 — readEvolveState/writeEvolveState logic repeated at lines 150-250 and 800-900. State mutation bugs could corrupt EVOLVE-STATE.json. Should extract evolve/state.js, evolve/scoring.js, evolve/discovery.js.

items.title: cmdEvolveDiscover function is 400+ lines
items.description: lib/evolve.js:1150-1550 — Codebase scanning, Claude API calls, fallback logic, and item classification all in one function with nested loops exceeding cyclomatic complexity threshold. Hard to debug or test individual phases.

items.title: Phase number parsing logic duplicated across modules
items.description: lib/roadmap.js:100-200 and lib/phase.js:50-100 — Phase number parsing/sorting (parseInt, padStart, regex patterns) is duplicated with subtly different patterns. Bug fixes must be applied in multiple places.

items.title: Config defaults merged redundantly in multiple callers
items.description: lib/utils.js, lib/tracker.js, lib/backend.js — Each module has its own config-loading with default-merging logic. A unified config-loader.js abstraction would eliminate duplication and normalize field precedence.

items.title: cmdDashboard (400+ lines) duplicates roadmap analysis logic from roadmap.js
items.description: lib/commands.js lines 907-1294 — dashboard parsing helpers (`parseDashboardShippedMilestones`, `parseDashboardActiveMilestones`, `parseDashboardPhases`) perform regex parsing over ROADMAP.md content that is already done by `analyzeRoadmap` in lib/roadmap.js. The helpers are private, untested, and undocumented.

items.title: Milestone parsing logic is duplicated across utils.js, paths.js, and roadmap.js
items.description: lib/utils.js lines 669-707 (`getMilestoneInfo`), lib/paths.js lines 98-123 (`parseRoadmapMilestone`), and lib/roadmap.js lines 73-94 each implement ROADMAP.md milestone parsing with different regex patterns. paths.js explicitly notes it duplicates utils.js to avoid a circular dep — extracting to a zero-dependency helper would fix this.

items.title: cmdPhaseRemove is 270 lines — split it
items.description: lib/phase.js cmdPhaseRemove (lines 315–585) does roadmap parsing, directory renaming, file renaming, frontmatter patching, and reference updating all in one function. Extract into: validateRemoveArgs(), reorderDirectories(), reorderRoadmapEntries(), patchFrontmatterRefs() to make each step testable independently.

items.title: cmdMilestoneComplete is 266 lines — split it
items.description: lib/phase.js cmdMilestoneComplete (lines 776–1041) interleaves archive copy, git operations, state patching, and roadmap rewriting. Extract into discrete steps with clear precondition checks at each boundary. Reduces cognitive load and makes each operation independently testable.

items.title: cmdDashboard is 150+ lines of mixed concerns
items.description: lib/commands.js cmdDashboard mixes data gathering, formatting, and output rendering inline. Extract a buildDashboardData() function returning a plain object and a renderDashboard(data) renderer so the logic is unit-testable without spawning CLI processes.

items.title: Phase directory lookup pattern duplicated 5+ times
items.description: The pattern `normalizePhaseName(phase) → dirs.find(d => d.startsWith(normalized))` appears identically in lib/phase.js (~lines 320, 420, 475, 595), lib/context.js, and lib/commands.js. Extract to lib/utils.js as findPhaseDir(phasesDir, phaseArg) with a consistent 'Phase not found' error.

items.title: 15 cmdInit* functions in context.js share boilerplate
items.description: lib/context.js has 15 cmdInit* functions (lines 200–1377) for different workflow initializations. Each function independently loads state, reads config, resolves paths, and builds JSON output with ~30 lines of shared boilerplate. Extract a buildInitContext(overrides) base function to reduce duplication by ~60%.

items.title: commands.js is 3,014 lines — too large to navigate or test
items.description: lib/commands.js lines 1–3014: The module exports 30+ functions across research, planning, execution, and analysis domains. A single 3K-line file increases test coupling, makes commands hard to locate, and slows incremental compilation.

items.title: runEvolve is a 225-line function with 3+ levels of nesting
items.description: lib/evolve.js lines 1804–2028: `runEvolve` mixes iteration engine, discovery coordination, and state management in one function. Extracting these responsibilities would make each independently testable and the overall flow readable.

items.title: analyzeCodebaseForItems handles 7 dimensions without helpers
items.description: lib/evolve.js lines 1165–1380: A 200+ line function with repetitive discovery logic for each dimension. Extracting a dimension-discoverer generator would eliminate duplication and make it trivial to add new dimensions.

items.title: Phase renumbering logic is 70 lines of deep nesting
items.description: lib/phase.js lines 406–475: `cmdPhaseRemove` handles integer and decimal phase renumbering in interleaved branches. Splitting these into separate helpers would eliminate off-by-one risk and improve readability.

items.title: Local flag() helper duplicates utils.js parseIncludeFlag
items.description: lib/commands.js lines 2197-2209: a local `flag()` function is defined that duplicates functionality already in utils.js (`parseIncludeFlag`). This creates a maintenance burden and inconsistent behavior. Import from utils.js and remove the local copy.

items.title: Phase duration parsing in roadmap.js breaks on multiline descriptions
items.description: lib/roadmap.js lines 96-120: Duration extraction uses a regex that doesn't handle newlines in phase descriptions. Phases with multi-paragraph descriptions lose their duration data silently. Improve the regex to handle `\n` within phase blocks.

items.title: TRACKER.md lookup is O(n) — no index
items.description: lib/tracker.js `loadTrackerMapping()`: phase/plan IDs are looked up with linear scans of TRACKER.md on every call. For projects with 100+ phases this is noticeably slow. Build an in-memory Map index on first load.

items.title: Replace 600-line routeCommand switch in bin/grd-tools.js with descriptor-based routing
items.description: bin/grd-tools.js routeCommand() starts at line 107 and contains 50+ case statements spanning ~600 lines. Every new command requires editing this monolith. Use a descriptor-based dispatch table like mcp-server.js already does.

items.title: lib/cleanup.js analyzeJsdocDrift() is 100+ lines with mixed concerns
items.description: lib/cleanup.js analyzeJsdocDrift() handles file scanning, JSDoc block parsing, parameter extraction, and mismatch detection all in one function. Also creates new RegExp objects inside loops. Extract _parseJsdocBlock(), _extractJsdocParams(), _extractFunctionParams() and compile patterns once.

items.title: lib/mcp-server.js COMMAND_DESCRIPTORS table (~500 lines) needs factory functions
items.description: lib/mcp-server.js lines ~200-700 contain a 500-line descriptor table with heavily repeated structure for similar commands (e.g., all tracker sync commands have identical shape). Introduce factory functions like makeStateCommand(), makePhaseCommand() to reduce duplication and make the table editable.

items.title: lib/tracker.js handlePrepareRoadmapSync() is 120+ lines with multiple concerns
items.description: lib/tracker.js handlePrepareRoadmapSync() parses milestones, phases, and schedule, then generates operations, all inline. Extract _parseAllMilestones(), _parseAllPhases(), _buildMilestoneOperations() to improve readability and testability.

items.title: lib/context.js is a 1377-line grab-bag of 15+ workflows
items.description: lib/context.js conflates cmdInitExecutePhase, cmdInitPlanPhase, cmdInitResume, and many other workflows into one file. Hard to modify one workflow without affecting others. Should be split into feature-specific context modules.

items.title: Duplicate directory recursion logic across 3+ modules
items.description: lib/cleanup.js (_walkDir), lib/worktree.js (getGrdWorktrees), and lib/context.js each implement their own directory traversal with slightly different logic. Should be consolidated into a shared utility in lib/utils.js to prevent inconsistent behavior.

items.title: spawnClaude and spawnClaudeAsync are nearly identical
items.description: In lib/autopilot.js (around lines 130–200), spawnClaude and spawnClaudeAsync share ~70 lines of duplicated logic differing only in async/await usage. Extract shared core logic to avoid sync bugs when changes are made to one but not the other.

items.title: Split lib/commands.js (3,014 lines) into domain modules
items.description: lib/commands.js contains 39 unrelated functions across todo management, config ops, history digest, model resolution, and progress rendering. Split into lib/commands/todo.js, lib/commands/config.js, lib/commands/progress.js, etc. Current size makes navigation and maintenance difficult.

items.title: Extract 20 init workflows from lib/context.js into lib/init/ subdirectory
items.description: lib/context.js (1,377 lines) packs 20 cmdInit* workflow functions. Each should live in lib/init/{workflow-name}.js. The file is also a circular dependency hub, importing from state.js, phase.js, roadmap.js, tracker.js, autopilot.js, evolve.js, and more.

items.title: bin/grd-tools.js help string is manually maintained and outdated
items.description: bin/grd-tools.js lines 144-148: the usage string in error() manually lists commands and omits `evolve`, `autopilot`, `worktree-hook-create`, and `worktree-hook-remove` (present in routeCommand at lines 742-747). items.title: cmdStateLoad raw output missing autonomous_mode, ceremony, and execution fields
items.description: lib/state.js lines 70-91: the --raw format outputs only 12 key=value pairs. Config fields added after initial implementation — autonomous_mode, use_teams, code_review_enabled, ceremony settings — are present in JSON output but absent from raw text. Commands that parse raw output with shell scripting will miss these fields, causing silent misconfiguration.

items.title: STATE.md error messages don't suggest corrective action
items.description: lib/state.js lines 131, 180, 223: multiple catch blocks call `error('STATE.md not found')` with no path hint and no recovery suggestion (e.g., 'run /grd:new-project or /grd:resume to initialize'). The error function at lib/utils.js lines 512-515 is already available — improve the message strings to be actionable.

items.title: cmdSearch only searches .planning/, missing commands/ and agents/ content
items.description: lib/commands.js lines 2627-2653: cmdSearch at line 2632 scans only .planning/ markdown files. Searching for 'token threshold' or 'ceremony level' returns no results if those terms only appear in commands/ or agents/ markdown. Add commands/ and agents/ to the search scope or accept a --path parameter.

items.title: Evolve discovery silently falls back to heuristics when Claude parse fails
items.description: lib/evolve.js lines 1232-1244 and 1291-1296: parseDiscoveryOutput() at line 1311 strips markdown fences and retries, but if Claude outputs explanatory text before/after the JSON, parse fails and returns [], triggering the static heuristic fallback at line 1291. The user has no way to know the fallback occurred. Log a warning when Claude-powered discovery falls back to heuristics.

items.title: analyzeComplexity() silently returns empty results when ESLint is unavailable
items.description: lib/cleanup.js lines 80-112: execFileSync('npx', ['eslint', ...]) at line 95 fails with a cryptic error if ESLint is not available. The catch block at line 102 returns [] silently. Users see no complexity violations — not because there are none, but because the tool isn't installed. Log a diagnostic when ESLint cannot run.

items.title: cmdInitResearchWorkflow accepts arbitrary workflow names without validation
items.description: lib/context.js (called from bin/grd-tools.js lines 543-551): cmdInitResearchWorkflow accepts any `wf` string and embeds it into output JSON without validation. While the CLI router validates via validateSubcommand, callers invoking the function directly (e.g., in tests or via MCP) can pass arbitrary workflow names. Add an allowed-workflows check inside the function for defense in depth.

items.title: Context init functions don't detect missing .planning/ directory
items.description: lib/context.js cmdInit* functions (all 20+) attempt to read .planning/ files without first checking if the directory exists. Users on fresh projects receive file-not-found errors instead of a helpful suggestion to run /grd:new-project.

items.title: Phase number error message doesn't explain expected format
items.description: lib/context.js line 97 produces: 'phase required for init execute-phase. Usage: init execute-phase <phase-number>'. Users don't know if phase-number should be '1', '01', '1.1', or '01.1'. Add a concrete example.

items.title: Phase add error message lacks format guidance
items.description: lib/phase.js line 128 produces: 'description required for phase add'. No example is shown. Should say: 'description required (e.g., grd phase add "Implement caching layer")'.

items.title: buildParallelContext() has no JSDoc despite complex signature
items.description: lib/parallel.js lines 55-66, buildParallelContext() takes 3 parameters and returns a complex object used by all parallel execution paths. No JSDoc exists. Undocumented parameter semantics make it hard for new contributors to use correctly.

items.title: inferCeremonyLevel() logic undocumented
items.description: lib/context.js line 46, inferCeremonyLevel() auto-selects 'light'|'standard'|'full' based on opaque signals. No JSDoc explains the thresholds. Users relying on auto-inference have no way to understand why a level was chosen.

items.title: Evolve discovery functions lack JSDoc
items.description: lib/evolve.js lines 450-500, discoverUsabilityItems(), discoverStabilityItems(), and related functions have no JSDoc. The discovery engine is complex and new; without documentation the intent of each function is unclear.

items.title: 15+ public functions in backend.js lack JSDoc
items.description: lib/backend.js — detectBackend, resolveBackendModel, getBackendCapabilities have inline comments but no @param/@returns blocks. IDEs can't autocomplete parameters; generated docs incomplete.

items.title: Error messages inconsistently actionable across CLI
items.description: lib/commands.js:100-200 and lib/phase.js:80-150 — Some errors include helpful context ('Run /grd:plan-phase 01 first'), others are terse ('Phase not found'). No consistent severity level naming or actionable guidance pattern.

items.title: --raw flag behavior undocumented with inconsistent formats
items.description: bin/grd-tools.js:100-200 — Many commands support --raw but usage is not documented in help text. Output formats vary (JSON, newline-separated, custom). Scripts and integrations break if format changes.

items.title: paths.js functions imported with inconsistent aliases
items.description: lib/paths.js — Functions like phasesDir, researchDir, todosDir are imported with inline aliases in 15+ places. No documentation on what each path resolves to relative to .planning/. Hard for contributors to understand directory structure.

items.title: cmdResolveModel silently returns 'sonnet' for unknown agent types in --raw mode
items.description: lib/commands.js lines 463-468 — when `agentType` is not in `MODEL_PROFILES`, the function returns `{ model: 'sonnet', unknown_agent: true }` in JSON mode but just `'sonnet'` in `--raw` mode with no warning. A typo in an agent name silently falls back to sonnet.

items.title: flag() helper in grd-tools.js returns the next flag as a value when flag value is missing
items.description: bin/grd-tools.js lines 131-134 — `flag(args, name, fallback)` returns `args[i+1]` without checking if it starts with `--`. For example, `state patch --field --value` would set the field name to `'--value'`. There is no guard producing a clear error message for this malformed invocation.

items.title: cmdHistoryDigest silently skips malformed SUMMARY.md files with no file path logged
items.description: lib/commands.js lines 424-426 — the inner loop catches parse errors with `catch (e) { // Skip malformed summaries }` and no file path or error detail. The outer catch (line 442) gives only a generic message. Debugging a malformed summary requires manual bisection.

items.title: spawnClaudeAsync drops the error detail when the claude binary is missing
items.description: lib/autopilot.js line 216 — `child.on('error', ...)` sets `exitCode: 1` but ignores the error object. The caller (grd-tools.js line 738) catches only `err.message` from the outer rejection. If the `claude` binary is missing or not executable, the user gets no actionable diagnostic.

items.title: Generic 'Phase not found' errors lack recovery hints
items.description: Several commands (lib/phase.js:324, lib/commands.js multiple locations) emit bare 'Phase not found' or 'ROADMAP.md not found' with no actionable guidance. Replace with messages that show available phases, suggest running `grd state load` to diagnose, or indicate what the expected path should be.

items.title: MCP tool descriptions in mcp-server.js lack examples
items.description: lib/mcp-server.js (2,227 lines) registers 30+ tools with description strings. Many descriptions omit parameter examples, valid value ranges, or error conditions. LLM agents calling these tools without examples produce malformed inputs. Add concrete example values to each inputSchema description field.

items.title: Evolve discovery silently returns empty on JSON parse error
items.description: lib/evolve.js discoverWithClaude() parses Claude's response as JSON and catches parse errors silently, returning an empty improvement list. The user gets no feedback that discovery failed. Emit a warning with the raw response excerpt so users can diagnose prompt or model issues.

items.title: cmdPhaseInsert error says 'not found in ROADMAP' when phase is actually shipped
items.description: lib/phase.js lines 195–200: When a phase number exists but is already in the shipped section, the error says 'not found in ROADMAP' instead of 'not in active roadmap'. Users think the phase doesn't exist and re-add it.

items.title: cmdWorktreeRemove reports success when git still holds a reference
items.description: lib/worktree.js lines 316–322: `already_gone: true` is returned when the path doesn't exist on disk, but the function doesn't run `git worktree prune` to remove the stale git reference. Users believe cleanup is complete when it isn't.

items.title: Phase consistency check doesn't distinguish plan-complete vs has-summaries
items.description: lib/phase.js lines 606–680: `cmdValidateConsistency` detects orphaned phases but reports only counts, not whether plans are 'complete' (all summaries match plans) vs 'in-progress' (some summaries present). Reduces actionability of output.

items.title: cmdGenerateSlug doesn't guarantee ASCII-safe output
items.description: lib/commands.js lines 70–85: Generated slugs pass through Unicode normalization but don't strip non-ASCII characters. Slugs used in file paths can produce system-specific results on filesystems with different Unicode normalization modes.

items.title: DIMENSION_WEIGHTS constants have no rationale comments
items.description: lib/evolve.js lines 100–180: `DIMENSION_WEIGHTS`, `EFFORT_MODIFIERS`, and `SOURCE_MODIFIERS` are defined without comments explaining the tuning rationale. Contributors can't reason about whether adjusting a weight is correct.

items.title: grd-tools.js help text is hardcoded and incomplete
items.description: bin/grd-tools.js lines 145-147: help output is a static string that doesn't reflect the ~40+ commands actually implemented. Users who run `grd-tools --help` see an incomplete list. Generate help text dynamically from the command routing table.

items.title: postinstall.js creates directories silently
items.description: bin/postinstall.js lines 43-67: directories are created without any log output. Users can't tell if postinstall ran or was skipped. Add per-directory `console.log('  Created .planning/milestones/anonymous')` messages.

items.title: No guidance for recovering from corrupted STATE.md or orphaned phases
items.description: docs/ contains no troubleshooting guide. There is no documentation on recovering from a corrupted STATE.md, fixing orphaned phases after a failed phase add/remove, or resolving worktree conflicts. Add docs/troubleshooting.md with these common recovery scenarios.

items.title: scaffold.js silently falls back to default template on error
items.description: lib/scaffold.js lines 70-77: template selection errors are caught and silently fall back to 'standard'. Users don't know why their specified template was ignored. Change to output the error reason: `{ error: 'Failed to select template: ${e.message}' }` before fallback.

items.title: Dashboard regenerates all data synchronously on every call
items.description: lib/commands.js cmdDashboard and related: ROADMAP.md, STATE.md, and phase summaries are all parsed synchronously on each dashboard invocation. For large projects this creates noticeable lag. Add a TTL-based cache (30s) or a `--no-cache` flag.

items.title: Validation error messages lack actionable context
items.description: lib/utils.js lines 330-424: validation functions throw errors but the messages don't suggest how to fix the problem. E.g., 'Invalid phase number' with no indication of valid range. Add suggested fixes to each validation error: 'Phase N not found. Available phases: 1-5.'

items.title: lib/tracker.js error messages don't guide the user to fix the issue
items.description: lib/tracker.js line 448 outputs error('No tracker configured') without telling the user what to configure or where. Similarly, config parse failures return silently. Better: error('Tracker not configured. Run /grd:tracker-setup or set tracker.provider in .planning/config.json').

items.title: bin/grd-tools.js help text lists 50+ commands with no grouping
items.description: bin/grd-tools.js lines 78-80 output a flat list of all commands without grouping by domain (state, phase, scaffold, tracker, etc.) or including examples. Users cannot quickly find the command they need. Group by category and add one-line examples per command.

items.title: lib/evolve.js work item scoring algorithm is undocumented and DIMENSION_WEIGHTS unused
items.description: lib/evolve.js scoreWorkItem() applies weights to work items but DIMENSION_WEIGHTS is defined and never actually used in the prioritization logic. The scoring algorithm is not documented. Users cannot understand why certain items are selected. Document the algorithm and fix or remove the dead DIMENSION_WEIGHTS constant.

items.title: lib/context.js silently ignores backend detection failure
items.description: lib/context.js line 76 catches backend detection errors with no logging. When backend detection fails, the wrong model capabilities are assumed, leading to incorrect model selection in downstream commands. Should log a warning: 'Backend detection failed, defaulting to minimal capabilities'.

items.title: lib/mcp-server.js tool descriptions lack parameter examples
items.description: COMMAND_DESCRIPTORS in lib/mcp-server.js defines tool descriptions but most lack input parameter examples (e.g., what a valid phase number looks like, what fields state patch accepts). Claude and other MCP clients struggle to form correct calls without examples. Add example values to all parameter descriptions.

items.title: lib/evolve.js core algorithms have no JSDoc
items.description: lib/evolve.js discovery engine, scoring logic (~line 200), and iteration/dependency merging (~line 400+) are documented only with sparse inline comments. The algorithms are non-obvious and would benefit greatly from JSDoc covering params, return values, and algorithm rationale.

items.title: lib/mcp-server.js (2227 lines) lacks architectural overview
items.description: lib/mcp-server.js has no module-level documentation explaining request/response patterns, tool registration strategy, or error handling approach. Maintainers have no way to understand the design without reading the entire file.

items.title: Merge conflicts in worktree abort with no actionable guidance
items.description: lib/worktree.js cmdWorktreeMerge: when a merge conflict causes an abort, the error is surfaced but no actionable guidance is given to the user (e.g., 'run git status in .worktrees/X to resolve manually'). User is left confused about next steps.

items.title: CLAUDE.md has no documentation for the evolve loop
items.description: CLAUDE.md documents all other workflows extensively but has no section for the evolve loop (work item dimensions, discovery algorithm, scoring, iteration count semantics). Users cannot understand how to tune the evolve loop or interpret its output without reading source code.

items.title: Destructive operations lack --dry-run flag
items.description: lib/phase.js:180-250 (cmdPhaseRemove) and lib/worktree.js:300-400 — cmdPhaseRemove, cmdWorktreeRemove, and file writes lack --dry-run option to preview changes before executing. Users can't safely verify what will be deleted.

items.title: Field normalization logic duplicated in cmdStatePatch and cmdStateUpdate
items.description: lib/state.js lines 143-183 vs 192-226: both functions contain identical 10-line blocks for 'try original field name, fall back to underscore-to-space mapping'. Extract into a shared `stateReplaceFieldSafe(content, field, value)` helper. The internal stateReplaceField already exists but only handles exact-match replacement.

items.title: lib/paths.js duplicates milestone-parsing from utils.js due to circular dependency
items.description: lib/paths.js lines 65-123: inferMilestoneFromDisk() and parseRoadmapMilestone() are explicit duplicates of getMilestoneInfo() in lib/utils.js lines 669-707. Comments at lines 59 and 93 acknowledge this: 'Duplicated here to avoid circular dependency: utils.js imports paths.js.' Fix the circular dependency by moving getMilestoneInfo from utils.js to paths.js and importing it from there.

items.title: Custom YAML parser in lib/frontmatter.js mishandles edge cases
items.description: lib/frontmatter.js lines 19-100: the manual stack-based YAML parser handles basic keys, inline arrays, and list items but not multi-level nested objects, quoted strings with colons, or multi-line values. Frontmatter is a core data format used in PLAN.md, SUMMARY.md, command files, and agent files. Consider replacing with js-yaml (already common in Node.js) or substantially expanding the parser's test coverage.

items.title: lib/tracker.js internal handlers not exported, limiting unit testability
items.description: lib/tracker.js lines 480, 523, 561: handleSyncRoadmap, handleSyncPhase, handleUpdateStatus, handleAddComment, handleSyncStatus, and handleSchedule are private to cmdTracker dispatch and have no JSDoc. Tracker sync failures are silent by design but bugs in these handlers cause data loss. Exporting and documenting them would allow direct unit testing without going through the full dispatch.

items.title: discoverQualityItems() only checks tests/unit/, causing false positives
items.description: lib/evolve.js lines 362-388: the test-file discovery at line 374 only checks `tests/unit/{file}.test.js`. Tests at `tests/*.test.js` (e.g., tests/backend.test.js, tests/commands.test.js) are ignored. This causes false positives — lib/backend.js already has tests/backend-real-env.test.js but discoverQualityItems would still flag it as missing a test file.

items.title: lib/backend.js model name table contains non-existent model identifiers
items.description: lib/backend.js lines 39-47: the DEFAULT_BACKEND_MODELS table maps to `gpt-5.3-codex`, `gemini-3-pro`, and `claude-opus-4-5` / `claude-sonnet-4-5` / `claude-haiku-4-5`. Several of these are not publicly known identifiers as of early 2026. Stale model names cause API errors for non-Claude backends. Add a comment with the date the table was last verified and provide a config override mechanism.

items.title: mcp-server.js branch coverage at 55%
items.description: jest.config.js line 19 sets the branch coverage threshold for lib/mcp-server.js at 55% — the lowest in the codebase. The MCP server handles all JSON-RPC dispatch and error paths; uncovered branches leave failure modes untested.

items.title: phase.js branch coverage at 70%
items.description: jest.config.js sets branch coverage for lib/phase.js at 70%. Phase lifecycle operations (add/insert/remove/complete) are critical; edge cases like inserting into a single-phase roadmap or removing the active phase are likely untested.

items.title: tracker.js branch coverage at 70%
items.description: jest.config.js sets branch coverage for lib/tracker.js at 70%. Tracker integration has multiple providers (github, mcp-atlassian, none) and migration paths; incomplete coverage could miss sync failure branches.

items.title: verify.js branch coverage at 70%
items.description: jest.config.js sets branch coverage for lib/verify.js at 70%. Verification gates are safety-critical; 70% branch coverage is too low for a module that gates phase completion and plan execution.

items.title: evolve.js branch coverage at 70%
items.description: jest.config.js sets branch coverage for lib/evolve.js at 70%. The evolve loop is new and complex; low branch coverage in discovery and work item generation increases risk of silent failures in the autonomous iteration loop.

items.title: scaffold.js branch coverage at 70%
items.description: jest.config.js sets branch coverage for lib/scaffold.js at 70%. Scaffolding errors that create malformed phase directories are difficult to detect after the fact; edge cases in template selection need higher coverage.

items.title: commands.js branch coverage at 70%
items.description: jest.config.js sets branch coverage for lib/commands.js at 70%. With 39 exported functions and complex dashboard rendering logic, the uncovered 30% likely includes error branches in cmdDashboard() and related parsing functions.

items.title: Missing tests for MCP error paths in captureExecution
items.description: lib/mcp-server.js:1868-1972 — captureExecution and captureExecutionAsync intercept process.exit but lack tests for malformed JSON responses, concurrent exits, and nested process calls. Silent failures won't propagate to MCP clients.

items.title: Sparse tests for phase add/insert/remove edge cases
items.description: lib/phase.js:100-300 — cmdPhaseInsert modifies ROADMAP.md at specific heading levels but tests for malformed headings, missing phases, and edge-case insertions are missing. Complex mutations can silently corrupt file structure.

items.title: No tests for tracker sync failure scenarios
items.description: lib/tracker.js:220-350 — GitHub/Jira sync via gh CLI has no tests for network timeouts, malformed configs, or partial sync states. Silent failures leave phase status out of sync without user visibility.

items.title: Config loaded without schema validation
items.description: lib/utils.js:100-250 — loadConfig accepts any JSON and fills in defaults without validating field values (e.g., ceremony level is valid enum, model_profile is supported). Typos in config.json silently use defaults without warning.

items.title: Phase name length and charset not validated
items.description: lib/phase.js:200-300 — normalizePhaseName accepts kebab-case slugs but doesn't validate max length (filesystem limits ~255 chars) or reject emoji/unicode. Very long slugs or special chars cause filesystem or ROADMAP parsing issues.

items.title: No round-trip integrity tests for markdown-split
items.description: lib/markdown-split.js:45-120 — readMarkdownWithPartials has no test that reassembly produces the original content. Silent data corruption in large ROADMAP/STATE file reassembly goes undetected.

items.title: Custom YAML parser in frontmatter.js fails on multi-line strings and embedded colons
items.description: lib/frontmatter.js lines 19-100 — the hand-rolled parser has no support for block scalars (`|`, `>`), multi-line strings, or quoted strings with embedded colons. A PLAN.md with `objective: "Fix the foo: bar issue"` would parse the colon inside quotes as a nested key separator.

items.title: cmdFindPhase duplicates findPhaseInternal from utils.js
items.description: lib/commands.js lines 482-536 re-implements phase directory discovery that already exists in utils.js `findPhaseInternal` (lines 545-620). The two implementations have diverged — bug fixes applied to one don't flow to the other.

items.title: cmdResolveModel returns raw tier name instead of backend-resolved model ID
items.description: lib/commands.js lines 455-473 — `cmdResolveModel` returns the tier string (e.g. `'sonnet'`) without calling `resolveBackendModel`. But `resolveModelInternal` and `resolveModelForAgent` in utils.js do call `resolveBackendModel`. The CLI `resolve-model` command gives different results than the model actually used by agents.

items.title: Git commit failure branch sets wrong reason 'nothing_to_commit'
items.description: lib/commands.js lines 588-594 — the `else` branch for a generic git commit failure (e.g. pre-commit hook rejection) sets `reason: 'nothing_to_commit'`, identical to the actual empty-index case above it. Agents reading this response cannot distinguish an empty index from a hook failure.

items.title: discoverImproveFeatureItems output-call regex generates false positives and negatives
items.description: lib/evolve.js lines 689-706 — the regex `output\(\s*(\w+)\s*,\s*raw\s*(?:,\s*raw\s*\?)?\s*\)` only matches single-identifier first arguments (misses object literals), and the follow-up check regex fails on multi-line calls. This heuristic produces unreliable discovery results.

items.title: cmdStateSnapshot raw output can emit 'NaN%' if Progress field contains a bar string
items.description: lib/state.js line 719 — `result.progress_percent` is derived from `parseInt(progressRaw.replace('%', ''), 10)`. If STATE.md Progress field contains a bar like `[██░░] 20%`, `parseInt` of the full bar text returns `NaN`, so the raw output becomes `"Phase 5 (active) - NaN%"`.

items.title: worktree.js hook-create/remove and push-pr paths have no unit test coverage
items.description: lib/worktree.js — `cmdWorktreeHookCreate` and `cmdWorktreeHookRemove` are only reachable via alias commands (grd-tools.js lines 742-746) and appear to have no test coverage in tests/unit/worktree.test.js. The `cmdWorktreePushAndPR` path is similarly untested at the unit level.

items.title: 327 JSDoc blocks missing @throws annotations
items.description: lib/phase.js, lib/commands.js, and lib/context.js have comprehensive @param/@returns JSDoc but zero @throws tags. Functions like cmdPhaseAdd, cmdPhaseRemove can throw multiple error conditions. Without @throws, callers have no contract for error handling.

items.title: TODO/FIXME markers indicating unfinished work
items.description: Multiple TODO/FIXME comments exist across lib/ that flag known deficiencies. Each represents deferred technical debt. Audit and either resolve or convert to tracked issues with /grd:add-todo so they don't get lost across sessions.

items.title: Error paths in lib/deps.js and lib/scaffold.js uncovered
items.description: tests/unit/deps.test.js and tests/unit/scaffold.test.js have good happy-path coverage but minimal tests for failure modes (e.g., npm install failure in deps.js, permission errors in scaffold.js). The per-file thresholds in jest.config.js may be masking branch coverage gaps on catch paths.

items.title: lib/parallel.js error propagation paths not tested
items.description: lib/parallel.js orchestrates concurrent agent execution. tests/unit/parallel.test.js focuses on success cases. Worker crash, timeout, and partial-failure scenarios (where some workers fail and others succeed) lack dedicated test coverage, making the parallel executor brittle under real failure conditions.

items.title: Golden tests use capture.sh but CI enforcement unclear
items.description: tests/golden/ uses a capture.sh script to regenerate snapshots but it's unclear if golden output mismatch fails CI or just warns. If capture.sh must be manually run to update snapshots, developers may commit code that silently changes CLI output. Add explicit golden-test diff check to the test suite that fails on mismatch.

items.title: mcp-server.js has only 55% branch coverage threshold
items.description: jest.config.js line 20: `mcp-server.js` has the lowest branch-coverage threshold in the project at 55%. MCP tool handlers likely have multiple untested error and edge-case branches, making regressions likely.

items.title: commands.js and tracker.js sit at 70% branch coverage
items.description: jest.config.js lines 13, 26: Both modules share the same low threshold. Complex command routing and multi-provider tracker integration lack tests for error paths, concurrent failures, and malformed input.

items.title: No test for git merge conflict in cmdMilestoneComplete
items.description: lib/phase.js lines ~780–1000: `cmdMilestoneComplete` contains git merge logic with an abort path, but the test suite never exercises `git merge --abort`. Merge conflict handling is entirely untested.

items.title: Removing a phase does not check dependent phases
items.description: lib/phase.js line ~331: `cmdPhaseRemove` doesn't check whether the target phase is referenced in other phases' `Depends on:` fields. Removing a dependency silently creates orphaned references in ROADMAP.md.

items.title: No integration tests for stale/locked worktree cleanup
items.description: tests/integration/ has no tests for worktree cleanup when worktrees are stale or locked by another process. These edge cases are common in CI environments. Add integration tests that simulate locked worktrees and verify graceful recovery.

items.title: No integration tests for tracker sync failures
items.description: tests/integration/ lacks tests for tracker sync network failures and retry behavior. The tracker is marked non-blocking but there is no test verifying the workflow continues when GitHub/Jira are unreachable. Add tests with mocked HTTP failures.

items.title: No integration tests for long-term roadmap refine/link/unlink
items.description: The refine, link, and unlink operations in lib/long-term-roadmap.js lack integration test coverage in tests/integration/. These are complex operations that modify multiple files. Add end-to-end tests for each subcommand.

items.title: jest.config.js requires manual threshold entry for each new file
items.description: jest.config.js lines 6-30: per-file coverage thresholds must be manually added for each new module. New files start at 0% until someone adds them. Invert the approach: set a global default threshold and maintain only an exemption list for files with known lower coverage.

items.title: lib/mcp-server.js branch coverage threshold too low at 55%
items.description: jest.config.js sets branch coverage for lib/mcp-server.js at only 55%. The MCP tool dispatch logic has many conditional branches that go untested, meaning tool dispatch failures could go undetected. Raise threshold to at least 75% and add tests for error paths in executeTool().

items.title: lib/tracker.js GitHub tracker error paths have no tests
items.description: createGitHubTracker() calls ghExec() but no test covers ghExec() timeout, malformed gh CLI output, or rate limit errors. These are real production failure modes. Add unit tests for lib/tracker.js error scenarios.

items.title: lib/autopilot.js timeout and kill-signal handling not tested
items.description: lib/autopilot.js spawnClaudeAsync() has timeout logic but unit tests do not verify timeout behavior or kill signal handling. A hung subprocess could block the entire autopilot loop indefinitely.

items.title: No schema validation for .planning/config.json
items.description: lib/context.js loadConfig() (and callers) return any parsed JSON without validating required fields or types. Invalid config silently produces wrong behavior (e.g., missing ceremony.default_level defaults to undefined). Add Joi/Zod schema validation with helpful error messages.

items.title: lib/frontmatter.js has no schema validation per file type
items.description: frontmatter get/set/merge operations in lib/frontmatter.js accept any YAML without validating required fields per document type (PLAN.md vs SUMMARY.md vs RESEARCH.md). Invalid frontmatter propagates silently and can break downstream tools like verify plan-structure.

items.title: No integration test for lib/markdown-split.js split/reassemble round-trip
items.description: lib/markdown-split.js handles large-file splitting and reassembly, but only unit tests exist. No test verifies that split→reassemble produces identical output for real large planning files. A corruption bug in multi-part reassembly would be silent.

items.title: tests/golden/ snapshots have no documented update procedure
items.description: tests/golden/ captures CLI output via capture.sh but there is no documented or automated way to update golden snapshots when CLI output intentionally changes. Contributors must manually update snapshots, which is error-prone. Add npm run test:golden:update and document in CLAUDE.md.

items.title: lib/parallel.js wave failure aggregation behavior not covered by tests
items.description: lib/parallel.js handles wave-based parallel plan execution with failure aggregation, but integration tests don't cover the case where some plans in a wave succeed and others fail. The partial-success + continue-on-error path has no test coverage, risking silent data loss.

items.title: bin/postinstall.js has no unit tests
items.description: The critical postinstall script that creates directories during npm install has no corresponding test file in tests/. Directory creation failures would only be caught in live CI runs, not during development.

items.title: Evolve loop has no end-to-end integration tests
items.description: tests/integration/ contains no tests covering the evolve workflow's discovery engine, scoring logic, and work item lifecycle. This is one of the most complex orchestration flows in the codebase (lib/evolve.js) and is entirely untested at the integration level.

items.title: lib/cleanup.js calls findJsFiles once per analysis type
items.description: lib/cleanup.js analyzeFileSize, analyzeComplexity, analyzeExportConsistency, and analyzeDocStaleness each call findJsFiles independently. On large projects this means 4–6 full directory walks per quality check. findJsFiles result should be computed once and passed to all analyzers.

items.title: No check for duplicate phase numbers in add/insert
items.description: lib/phase.js cmdPhaseAdd and cmdPhaseInsert do not verify that the new phase number doesn't already exist in ROADMAP.md. Duplicate phase numbers cause crashes downstream in roadmap parsing and context init.

items.title: Quality analysis results are not deduplicated
items.description: lib/cleanup.js runQualityAnalysis: analyzeComplexity may report the same function violation multiple times (e.g., from different ESLint passes). Cleanup plans end up with duplicate tasks, which is confusing and leads to redundant work.
